metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the file (e.g., `.github/workflows/release-lint.yml` or a `make lint-release-pins` target) and its CI trigger in the Scope section and in AC1/AC2, so an executor knows exactly where to create the lint step and how it is invoked.
findings.finding: The 'new, self-contained workflow-lint/grep check' that enforces AC1 (SHA-pin) and AC2 (persist-credentials) is never given a file name or invocation path in the Scope or AC sections, so an executor cannot locate it in the repo, add it to CI, or cite it in the PR.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 and AC2; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: '`.github/workflows/release-lint.yml` (new) — triggered on `pull_request` for changes to `release.yml`; greps `release.yml` for tag/branch `uses:` references and for `actions/checkout` blocks missing `persist-credentials: false`.' Reference this file by name in AC1 and AC2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for each of the three required doc items in AC7 — e.g., add `grep -q 'MCP publisher' docs/releasing.md` for item (c) and `grep -q 'full-SHA' docs/releasing.md` (or equivalent anchor) for item (a), or consolidate into a single script that checks all three.
findings.finding: AC7 requires the `docs/releasing.md` 'Pinning' section to cover three items — (a) rationale, (b) lock-regen command, (c) MCP-publisher update instructions — but the only specified proving command (`grep -q "pip-compile --generate-hashes" docs/releasing.md`) checks only item (b); items (a) and (c) have no verification step.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC7
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the single grep in AC7 with three assertions run in sequence: `grep -q 'pip-compile --generate-hashes' docs/releasing.md && grep -q 'MCP publisher' docs/releasing.md && grep -qiE 'full.?SHA|commit SHA' docs/releasing.md`, and add these to the same release-lint workflow named in AC1/AC2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the SHA-pin lint and the `--no-isolation` enforcement lint to the Scope file list, specifying whether they live in a new workflow file (e.g. `.github/workflows/release-lint.yml`), as a job in `release.yml`, or elsewhere.
findings.finding: The SHA-pin lint ('a new, self-contained workflow-lint/grep check' in AC1/AC2) and the --no-isolation enforcement lint ('a workflow lint/test step that fails if --no-isolation is removed' in AC4) are described as concrete deliverables in What and ACs but are absent from the Scope section's enumerated file list, leaving the implementer with no assigned home for these artifacts.
findings.location: Scope section (file list) vs. AC1/AC2/AC4 and What section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: '`.github/workflows/release-lint.yml` (new) — PR-triggered workflow that (a) greps every `uses:` in `release.yml` for a 40-hex SHA and fails if any tag/branch is found, (b) greps `release.yml` for `persist-credentials: false` on every checkout block, and (c) greps `release.yml` for `--no-isolation` and fails if absent.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the concrete file path for the workflow-lint/grep check (e.g. `.github/workflows/release-lint.yml` or a new `tests/unit/test_release_workflow_pins.py`) to the Scope section, and confirm all three ACs point to the same file/mechanism.
findings.finding: The Scope section never names the file (workflow, script, or step location) for the 'new, self-contained workflow-lint/grep check' that ACs 1, 2, and 4 each designate as their proving mechanism. Without a concrete file target, an implementer has no grounded edit target and the AC verification steps have no home.
findings.location: Scope section; AC1, AC2, AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: '`.github/workflows/release-lint.yml` (new, PR-triggered on changes to `release.yml`) — the workflow-lint check that enforces ACs 1, 2, and 4 (SHA-pin, persist-credentials, --no-isolation); alternatively add `tests/unit/test_release_workflow_pins.py` if the lint is implemented as a pytest test (it would then be picked up by the existing parity mechanism).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact workflow file (e.g., `.github/workflows/lint-release.yml` or an existing CI workflow) that will host the `--no-isolation` lint/grep step, and specify its `on:` trigger so the AC is unambiguously verifiable.
findings.finding: The workflow-lint step that enforces `--no-isolation` is referenced in both the 'What' narrative and AC4, but its host workflow file is never named — it could be a separate lint workflow, part of `test-mcp-verify.yml`, or a step in an existing CI workflow — leaving implementers without a concrete home for this rule.
findings.location: What section (Hatchling enforcement bullet) and AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: '`.github/workflows/lint-release.yml` (new or extended) — hosts both the SHA-pin grep check, the `persist-credentials: false` grep check, and the `--no-isolation` grep check; triggered on pull_request paths: [`.github/workflows/release.yml`, `.github/release-requirements.txt`]'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC7's verification to cover all three required doc sections: add grep checks for a section heading covering MCP publisher update instructions and for rationale content (e.g. a phrase like 'immutable' or 'supply chain'), or acknowledge that (a) and (c) are verified by human review during the Gerrit gate.
findings.finding: AC7 requires `docs/releasing.md` to contain three items — (a) rationale for full-SHA action pins, (b) the pip-compile regeneration command, and (c) how to update the MCP publisher pin — but its own verification step (`grep -q "pip-compile --generate-hashes" docs/releasing.md`) only mechanically confirms item (b), leaving (a) and (c) with no stated verification mechanism.
findings.location: AC7 (docs releasing.md AC)

findings.finding: AC4 states that 'a workflow lint/test step fails if `--no-isolation` is removed' but gives no concrete proving command for this lint, unlike AC1/AC2 (grep) and AC7 (grep). The mechanism — whether a grep, a Python script, or something else — is left to the executor, and the AC cannot be independently verified without knowing what command to run.
findings.location: AC4 — 'with a workflow lint/test step that fails if `--no-isolation` is removed (no free-resolution path remains)'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC4: '— verified by `grep -q -- "--no-isolation" .github/workflows/release.yml` (part of the same lint step as AC1/AC2, so a single lint script checks all three).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the evidence artifact name to a single canonical string (remove 'e.g.') and specify it as a named constant shared across all three contributing workflow jobs.
findings.finding: The evidence artifact name is given as 'e.g. `release-evidence.txt`', leaving the canonical name undefined. Multiple independently-written workflow steps (SHA-pin lint, build step, mcp_verify step) must all write to the same named artifact; an 'e.g.' is an underspecified enum value that silently allows divergence.
findings.location: AC6 — Release evidence AC
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'e.g. `release-evidence.txt`' with a fixed name such as `release-evidence.txt` (no 'e.g.'), and add a one-line note: 'all contributing steps reference this exact artifact name; changing it requires updating all three jobs atomically'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the `pip install` command between the What section and AC3 — add `--no-deps` to the What section's description and add a note that `pip-compile --generate-hashes` must include all transitive dependencies for `--no-deps` to succeed.
findings.finding: AC3's install command includes `--no-deps` (`pip install --require-hashes --no-deps -r .github/release-requirements.txt`) but the What section's install command omits `--no-deps` (`pip install --require-hashes -r .github/release-requirements.txt`), creating a discrepancy: an implementer following What will produce a different (and potentially less safe) install invocation than an implementer following AC3.
findings.location: What section ('pre-install the hash-locked env') vs. AC3
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, change the install command to `pip install --require-hashes --no-deps -r .github/release-requirements.txt` and add: 'The lock file must include all transitive dependencies (pip-tools does this by default); `--no-deps` prevents pip from resolving anything outside the lock.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the cross-job evidence-accumulation mechanism (e.g., each job uploads a job-scoped fragment artifact, then a final `aggregate-evidence` job downloads all fragments and concatenates them into `release-evidence.txt`), or clarify that a single job owns the artifact and others pass data via job outputs.
findings.finding: The release-evidence.txt accumulation strategy across multiple GHA jobs running on separate ephemeral VMs is unspecified. The plan states that the SHA-pin lint 'dumps resolved action SHAs', the build step 'appends tool-versions.txt', and the mcp_verify step 'appends the publisher version + verified digest' — but in GitHub Actions each job runs on a fresh runner and cannot directly append to another job's artifact without an explicit download-append-re-upload pattern or a dedicated merge/aggregation job. Without specifying this mechanism, the evidence artifact will be incomplete (each job overwrites rather than appends) or the AC is unverifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Release evidence records action SHAs, build-tool versions, and the MCP publisher version + verified digest — each written by a named workflow step into a single evidence artifact'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a terminal `aggregate-evidence` job that `needs: [build, mcp_verify, mcp_registry]`, downloads each job's named fragment artifact (e.g., `sha-pin-evidence.txt`, `tool-versions.txt`, `mcp-publisher-evidence.txt`), concatenates them, and uploads the merged `release-evidence.txt`. Alternatively, use `actions/upload-artifact` with `overwrite: false` and unique artifact names per job, then document which artifact constitutes the canonical evidence bundle.

findings.finding: The `release-evidence.txt` artifact is described as being written to ('appended') by multiple separate jobs/steps (SHA-pin lint, build step, mcp_verify job), but GitHub Actions artifacts are not appendable across job boundaries — uploading to the same artifact name from a later job overwrites rather than merges, making the evidence record potentially lossy and the ownership of the artifact's final state undefined.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC for 'Release evidence records action SHAs, build-tool versions, and the MCP publisher version + verified digest'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the 'single artifact appended by multiple jobs' model with one of: (a) each contributing job uploads a distinct artifact (e.g., `lint-evidence.txt`, `build-evidence.txt`, `mcp-verify-evidence.txt`) and a final `assemble-evidence` job (needs: all prior jobs) downloads them all, concatenates them, and uploads `release-evidence.txt`; or (b) serialise all evidence-writing steps into a single job that runs last and has `needs:` on every other job, collecting outputs via job outputs or environment files. Document the chosen ownership model in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the exact grep/awk patterns (or a reference to a named script file) for both the SHA-pin check and the `persist-credentials: false` check in AC1/AC2, anchoring the SHA pattern with word-boundary guards and the credentials check with multi-line context (e.g., `awk` block-scan).
findings.finding: The grep patterns for the SHA-pin and `persist-credentials` checks are not specified, creating a real risk of substring false-positives or false-negatives (defect taxonomy #1 and #4): a pattern like `grep 'uses:.*@v'` misses branch-based refs; checking for `persist-credentials: false` on a single grep line can false-positive if the key appears in a comment or a different block.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 and AC2
findings.suggested_fix: For AC1: `grep -P 'uses:\s+[^@]+@(?![0-9a-f]{40})[^\s]' .github/workflows/release.yml && exit 1 || exit 0` (exit 1 if any non-SHA ref found). For AC2: use an awk block that scans from `uses: actions/checkout` to the next non-indented line, asserting `persist-credentials: false` appears within the block.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the byte-corruption method in the `test-mcp-verify.yml` description — e.g., `printf '\x00' | dd of=mcp-publisher.tar.gz bs=1 seek=100 conv=notrunc` — so the test unambiguously exercises single-byte tamper detection.
findings.finding: The `test-mcp-verify.yml` workflow is required to 'corrupt one byte' of the pinned archive as its core test mechanism, but neither the plan's What section nor the Scope section specifies how the corruption is performed, leaving the executor to pick a method that may silently be a no-op (e.g., truncating to zero bytes instead of flipping one byte, which some `sha256sum` implementations might handle differently).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5; Scope — `.github/workflows/test-mcp-verify.yml`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope bullet for `test-mcp-verify.yml`: 'the corruption step uses `printf "\\x00" | dd of=mcp-publisher.tar.gz bs=1 seek=100 conv=notrunc` to flip one byte at offset 100 without changing the file size.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify or replace the bespoke grep-based workflow-lint check (ACs 1 and 2) with an established tool (`zizmor` covers unpinned action refs and persist-credentials natively); if grep is retained, document why `zizmor` was rejected.
findings.finding: The plan mandates a bespoke 'new, self-contained workflow-lint/grep check' (ACs 1 and 2) using raw grep/shell logic to enforce SHA-pin and persist-credentials requirements, reinventing detection functionality that `zizmor` — a dedicated, actively maintained GitHub Actions security linter — already provides for exactly these two issue classes (unpinned action refs, persisted credentials). The plan offers no justification for building a hand-rolled detector instead of adopting the purpose-built tool.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('verified by a new, self-contained workflow-lint/grep check') and AC2 ('verified by the same workflow-lint step … which also greps')
findings.suggested_fix: Add `zizmor` (or `actionlint`) to the lint/pre-commit step and configure it to check `release.yml`; its `unpinned-uses` and `ref-confusion` / `persist-credentials` rules satisfy ACs 1 and 2 without a hand-rolled detector. If a custom grep check is kept for narrow scope (e.g., to avoid a new tool dependency), add a comment in the plan acknowledging the trade-off.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Provide a positive rationale in the plan for choosing bespoke grep over `zizmor` (or replace the grep-based lint with `zizmor .github/workflows/release.yml` in the lint/verification step, which covers ACs 1 and 2 with YAML-AST correctness and no additional maintenance burden).
findings.finding: The plan mandates a bespoke 'workflow-lint/grep check' (ACs 1 and 2) for SHA-pin and persist-credentials enforcement, but provides no positive rationale for choosing grep over `zizmor` — a purpose-built, YAML-AST-aware GitHub Actions security linter that natively detects unpinned action refs and missing persist-credentials, is actively maintained, and is already referenced in the repo's review cache as a clearly superior alternative. Grep is string-based and fragile to YAML formatting edge cases (e.g. inline comments containing old tags, future multi-line `uses:` blocks), whereas zizmor parses the YAML AST and understands GitHub Actions semantics. The plan's stated scope constraint (release.yml only) is fully achievable with zizmor via path filtering (`zizmor .github/workflows/release.yml`), so the narrower-scope argument does not distinguish grep from zizmor.
findings.location: What section ('a new, self-contained workflow-lint/grep check'); AC1; AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'a new, self-contained workflow-lint/grep check' in ACs 1/2 with: 'verified by running `zizmor .github/workflows/release.yml` (pinned to a specific version + SHA in the lint step), which natively detects non-SHA `uses:` references and missing `persist-credentials: false`; add a `pip-compile`-generated or direct SHA-pinned invocation of zizmor to the PR-triggered lint workflow.' If grep is deliberately preferred (e.g., to avoid a new tool dependency), add a brief rationale sentence explaining why.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before committing to a bespoke grep-based SHA-pin/persist-credentials lint, evaluate whether `zizmor` or `actionlint` can serve as the enforcement mechanism (or be called from the lint step), documenting why a custom grep is preferred if those tools are rejected.
findings.finding: The plan mandates a bespoke 'new, self-contained workflow-lint/grep check' to enforce SHA-pin and persist-credentials requirements in release.yml (ACs 1 and 2), reinventing functionality that `zizmor` (a dedicated, actively-maintained GitHub Actions security linter) and `actionlint` already provide out of the box — without acknowledging or dismissing either tool.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1 and AC2; What section ('a new, self-contained workflow-lint/grep check')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the bespoke grep lint with a `zizmor audit .github/workflows/release.yml` call (or `actionlint`) in the lint step — both can be pinned to a specific version in CI, produce machine-readable output, and cover the same SHA-pin + persist-credentials properties with broader pattern coverage and upstream maintenance.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `modelcontextprotocol/registry` GitHub releases ship a `checksums.txt` asset, and update the plan to resolve the 'prefer upstream checksums.txt' conditional into a definitive decision (either: 'upstream ships one at <url>' or 'upstream does not; hard-code the SHA-256').
findings.finding: The plan states 'Prefer an upstream checksums.txt if modelcontextprotocol/registry ships one; otherwise hard-code the SHA-256' — but never verifies whether the modelcontextprotocol/registry GitHub releases actually ship a checksums.txt file, leaving this as an unverified capability assertion that affects which implementation path the author takes.
findings.location: What section — MCP publisher subsection: 'Prefer an upstream checksums.txt if modelcontextprotocol/registry ships one'

findings.finding: The lock file path is specified only as 'e.g. under `.github/` or `requirements/`', leaving the concrete path as a decision for the executor rather than a resolved plan choice. This causes AC3's proving command (`pip install --require-hashes --no-deps -r <lock>`) to reference a placeholder `<lock>` with no canonical path, and means two developers could independently pick different locations.
findings.location: Scope section — 'A new release build lock/constraints file (e.g. under `.github/` or `requirements/`)'; AC3 — '`pip install --require-hashes --no-deps -r <lock>`'
findings.suggested_fix: Choose and record one path — e.g. `.github/release-requirements.txt` — in the Scope section and replace all `<lock>` occurrences in the ACs with that path. Update AC7's `pip-compile` command to include the full invocation: `pip-compile --generate-hashes --output-file=.github/release-requirements.txt .github/release-requirements.in`.

findings.finding: AC5's 'CI job that corrupts one byte of the pinned archive, runs the verify step, and asserts it exits non-zero' is described as a verification mechanism but is not listed in the Scope section as a file/workflow to create. A new CI job in an unnamed workflow file is a concrete deliverable that would be missed if an executor follows the scope bullets alone.
findings.location: AC5 — 'Verified by a CI job that corrupts one byte of the pinned archive…'; Scope section (no mention of a test workflow for the corruption check)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: '`.github/workflows/test-mcp-verify.yml` (new) — a PR-triggered workflow that downloads a test copy of the pinned archive, corrupts one byte, runs the `mcp_verify` sha256sum step, and asserts exit code is non-zero; triggered on changes to `release.yml`.'

findings.finding: The `mcp_verify` → `mcp_registry` artifact handoff will silently lose the `mcp-publisher` binary's executable bit: `actions/upload-artifact` v3+ does not preserve POSIX file permissions, so `mcp_registry`'s `./mcp-publisher login` call will fail with 'Permission denied' at runtime. The plan never mentions the need for a `chmod +x` step after downloading the artifact in `mcp_registry`.
findings.location: What section — 'Security contract — structural job split'; AC5 (mcp_registry job consumes the verified artifact)
findings.suggested_fix: In the `mcp_registry` job, after `actions/download-artifact` downloads the verified binary, add a step: `run: chmod +x mcp-publisher`. Document this in the plan's What section under the 'structural job split' description.

findings.finding: `actions/upload-artifact` (v3+/v4) does NOT preserve POSIX file permissions — the executable bit on the extracted `mcp-publisher` binary will be lost when `mcp_verify` uploads it, so `mcp_registry`'s `./mcp-publisher login github-oidc` will fail with a permission-denied error unless a `chmod +x` step is added after download.
findings.location: What — MCP publisher section / AC5 (`mcp_verify` job uploads verified artifact; `mcp_registry` consumes it)
findings.suggested_fix: In the `mcp_registry` job spec, insert a step between the artifact download and the `login` step: `run: chmod +x mcp-publisher`. Alternatively, note in the plan that the `actions/upload-artifact`/`download-artifact` pair strips executable bits and that restoration is required.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add pip-tools (or uv, if chosen as the alternative) to `pyproject.toml [dev]` dependencies at an exact or minimum pinned version, and document the regeneration command including the pip-tools install step in `docs/releasing.md`.
findings.finding: The plan specifies `pip-compile --generate-hashes` (pip-tools) as the lock-generation tool but pip-tools is not currently installed or listed as a dev dependency in `pyproject.toml`. The plan does not specify how pip-tools itself is installed or version-pinned in the dev/CI environment. An unversioned `pip install pip-tools` before generating the lock re-introduces the exact mutable-resolution problem the story is trying to solve.
findings.location: What section — 'Create a reviewed release lock/constraints file'; Scope — '.github/release-requirements.txt (new)'; AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"pip-tools>=7"` (or an exact pin) to `[project.optional-dependencies] dev` in `pyproject.toml`, so `pip install -e '.[dev]'` gives contributors a reproducible `pip-compile`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a decision rule (in the 'What' section or `docs/releasing.md`) specifying: (a) how to determine whether upstream ships a checksums.txt, (b) which takes precedence if both are available, and (c) what to do on pin-updates if the availability changes.
findings.finding: The 'prefer upstream checksums.txt, otherwise hard-code SHA-256' decision rule for the MCP publisher pin lacks a concrete decision protocol: no criterion specifies who determines whether upstream ships a checksums.txt, when that determination is made (at implementation time? at every pin-update?), or what the fallback procedure is if checksums.txt disappears after the initial pin.
findings.location: What section — MCP publisher bullet, 'Prefer an upstream checksums.txt' sentence
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'At each pin-update, run `curl -fsSL <release-url>/checksums.txt` — if HTTP 200, use upstream checksum and record the checksums.txt URL in the workflow comment; if 404, compute `sha256sum` locally and hard-code with comment `# upstream checksums.txt absent as of <date>`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/recovery procedure for the `--no-isolation` build approach — e.g., document in `docs/releasing.md` how to temporarily restore isolated builds (with the required two-reviewer approval to bypass the lint gate) while a lock-file fix is prepared, or provide a `make release-build-emergency` target that skips the lint.
findings.finding: The plan switches the build command from the default isolated `python -m build` to `python -m build --no-isolation` — a behavioral change that alters how the build environment is constructed — but provides no rollback or back-out plan if the `--no-isolation` approach causes build failures (e.g., an incomplete lock file, a package requiring isolation, or a Hatchling plugin that relies on the isolated env).
findings.location: What section ('Hatchling enforcement — decision') and AC4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the minimum mcp-publisher version that introduced `login github-oidc` (or verify the subcommand exists in the chosen pin target) so the executor pins at or above a known-working version.
findings.finding: The plan instructs pinning the mcp-publisher to an 'exact version' but never states a minimum version requirement for the `login github-oidc` subcommand. The current workflow downloads `releases/latest`, where `github-oidc` is known to work. If the pinned version is older than when that subcommand was introduced, `./mcp-publisher login github-oidc` will fail at release time with a command-not-found or usage error — silently defeating the OIDC authentication step.
findings.location: What section — MCP publisher download → verify → execute; AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the What section: 'Pin at or above the version currently served by releases/latest (confirm via `mcp-publisher --version` or the GitHub releases page before pinning); if the upstream changelog does not document when `login github-oidc` was introduced, treat the current latest as the minimum floor.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete procedure for obtaining the pinned SHA-256 when no upstream checksums.txt exists (e.g., `curl -fsSL <exact-version-url> -o mcp-publisher.tar.gz && sha256sum mcp-publisher.tar.gz`) so the executor records the archive hash, not the extracted binary hash.
findings.finding: The plan says 'Prefer an upstream `checksums.txt` if `modelcontextprotocol/registry` ships one' but does not verify whether exact-version GitHub release assets for this project actually include a `checksums.txt` (or equivalent). If no such file exists in a given release, the implementer falls back to hard-coding a SHA-256 — but the plan provides no guidance on HOW to obtain the SHA-256 for an exact-version archive (e.g., which curl command, whether to compute it from the downloaded file or look it up). This is an actionable gap for the executor.
findings.location: What section — MCP publisher download → verify → execute; 'Arch note'; AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'If no upstream checksums.txt exists, compute the archive digest with: `curl -fsSL "<exact-version-url>" -o mcp-publisher.tar.gz && sha256sum mcp-publisher.tar.gz` — record the hex digest of the `.tar.gz` archive (not the extracted binary) as the pinned value.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the `docs/releasing.md` 'Pinning' section AC to explicitly require documentation of: (a) the `--no-isolation` rationale (why PEP 517 isolation is disabled), and (b) why `pyproject.toml [build-system]` is intentionally left without a Hatchling version pin — verified by the same grep/lint step or a separate assertion.
findings.finding: The `--no-isolation` build strategy and the deliberate decision NOT to pin Hatchling in `pyproject.toml` are novel, non-standard architectural choices (deviating from PEP 517 isolated builds), but the `docs/releasing.md` Pinning section AC mandates only three items (SHA-pin rationale, lock-regen command, MCP pin update) and does NOT require the `--no-isolation` rationale or the pyproject.toml non-change decision to be documented anywhere. A future maintainer who removes the `--no-isolation` flag (bypassing the lint guard) or edits `pyproject.toml` [build-system] without understanding this constraint may silently revert to free-resolution builds.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `docs/releasing.md` bullet; What section — 'Hatchling enforcement — decision' paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC bullet for `docs/releasing.md`: '(d) the rationale for `--no-isolation` (why PEP 517 isolation is disabled and how Hatchling version is enforced instead), and (e) why `pyproject.toml [build-system]` is intentionally unpinned in this design.' Update the lint grep to also assert `grep -q 'no-isolation' docs/releasing.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add retry/timeout flags to the MCP publisher `curl` command (e.g., `--retry 3 --retry-delay 5 --connect-timeout 30`) and document the failure behavior when all retries are exhausted (abort with non-zero exit, which is correct, but should be explicit).
findings.finding: The curl download of the MCP publisher archive has no retry or backoff specified. A transient HTTP failure (GitHub releases rate-limit, CDN blip, DNS timeout) during a time-sensitive production release would leave the release blocked with no recovery path short of re-triggering the entire workflow from scratch.
findings.location: What section — MCP publisher step 1: `curl -fsSL "<exact-version-url>" -o mcp-publisher.tar.gz`
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `curl -fsSL "<url>" -o mcp-publisher.tar.gz` with `curl -fsSL --retry 3 --retry-delay 5 --connect-timeout 30 "<url>" -o mcp-publisher.tar.gz` and add a note in `docs/releasing.md` that a persistent failure means the upstream release has been removed or the URL/hash pair needs updating.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a PR-triggered CI step (or extend an existing workflow) that runs `pip install --require-hashes --no-deps -r .github/release-requirements.txt` in a dry-run / isolated venv on any pull request that modifies `.github/release-requirements.in` or `.github/release-requirements.txt`, so lock file breakage is caught before merge.
findings.finding: The PR-triggered test workflow (test-mcp-verify.yml) is scoped only to changes in release.yml; there is no CI trigger or test step that runs `pip install --require-hashes --no-deps -r .github/release-requirements.txt` on pull requests that modify `.github/release-requirements.in` or `.github/release-requirements.txt`. A regenerated or hand-edited lock file that is syntactically valid but hash-mismatched or dependency-incomplete would pass all PR checks and only fail at actual release time.
findings.location: Scope / `.github/release-requirements.txt` (new); AC: 'Release build tools install from .github/release-requirements.txt with --require-hashes'
findings.suggested_fix: In test-mcp-verify.yml (or a separate lightweight workflow), add a trigger `on: pull_request: paths: ['.github/release-requirements.in', '.github/release-requirements.txt']` with a job that creates a fresh venv and runs `pip install --require-hashes --no-deps -r .github/release-requirements.txt`; assert exit 0. This exercises the exact install path used in the release without needing OIDC or artifact access.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the Scope bullet for `docs/releasing.md`: update the existing `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1` tag reference in the 'Supply-chain attestations' section to reflect the SHA-pinned form (or note this as a deliberate exception with rationale).
findings.finding: docs/releasing.md line 45 already contains the verbatim mutable tag `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1` in its existing 'Supply-chain attestations' section. After this story SHA-pins that action in release.yml, that prose reference will be stale/inconsistent, but the Scope bullet restricts this story to only adding a new 'Pinning' section in docs/releasing.md — the existing stale tag reference in the 'Supply-chain attestations' section is not enumerated as an edit target.
findings.location: Scope section (docs/releasing.md bullet); docs/releasing.md line 45

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Scope whether the workflow-lint check will be implemented as a script in `scripts/` (requiring a matching entry in `gerrit-verify.yaml` or a parity-test exception) or as a standalone workflow (not subject to the parity test), and enumerate any resulting edit to `gerrit-verify.yaml` or `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py`.
findings.finding: The plan adds a 'new, self-contained workflow-lint/grep check' (ACs 1/2) that enforces constraints on release.yml. If this lint step is implemented as a script under `scripts/` or as a step inside `test.yml`, the existing `test_no_drift_script_gate_is_verified_only_in_branch_ci` test in `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` will require `gerrit-verify.yaml` to also invoke it — but `gerrit-verify.yaml` is not listed as an edit target in the plan's Scope, and is explicitly called out as out of scope.
findings.location: Scope section (Out of scope bullet); tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py lines 72-86
findings.suggested_fix: Implement the lint as a self-contained GitHub Actions workflow (e.g. `.github/workflows/release-lint.yml`) rather than a `scripts/*.py` call in `test.yml`, which sidesteps the parity-test constraint entirely.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
